key_insight: Chain-of-thought distillation from DeepSeek-R1 to a compact Qwen2.5-7B student improves math reasoning, but performance depends strongly on response length.
engineering_relevance: The study shows a practical LoRA/MLX pipeline on Apple Silicon for turning expensive reasoning traces into a deployable smaller model with measurable gains.
method: Built a CoT training set from 2011–2025 John O'Bryan Math Competition problems using a dual-agent framework
Fine-tuned Qwen2.5-7B with LoRA on Apple Silicon via MLX
Evaluated multiple short training runs and tested transfer on MATH-500 and controlled response-length settings
evidence_signal: Base Qwen2.5-7B scored 64.67% and the DeepSeek-R1 teacher 91.40%; the distilled student reached 69.43% mean accuracy on the competition set and 73.1% on MATH-500.
watch_item: An initial 1,000-iteration run overfit quickly, so the stable gains come from shorter 200-iteration runs and may not generalize across longer training schedules.
paper_title: Knowledge Distillation from Large Reasoning Models to Compact Student Models: A Case Study on the John O Bryan Mathematics Competition
